### Post by marcos.lohmann on 2012-01-04
Hi there,

I have a ubuntu vps I set to be my dmz node, and I connect my other ubuntu box (my tomcat server) to it through a pptp vpn. Both machines can ping each other over the 10.1.1.0 network.
Now I want to PORT FORWARD some requests (port 8080 essentially) from the dmz node to the tomcat server - it is not working... any thoughts?
 

$iptables-save

# Generated by iptables-save v1.4.4 on Tue Jan  3 07:47:35 2012
*nat
:PREROUTING ACCEPT [698:38520]
:POSTROUTING ACCEPT [1122:89354]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [1105:88470]
-A PREROUTING -p tcp -m tcp --dport 8080 -j DNAT --to-destination 10.1.1.155:8080
-A POSTROUTING -d 10.1.1.155/32 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 8080 -j SNAT --to-source 123.123.123.123
COMMIT
# Completed on Tue Jan  3 07:47:35 2012

:PREROUTING ACCEPT [8807:864273]
:INPUT ACCEPT [8671:857281]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [136:6992]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [8352:1180725]
:POSTROUTING ACCEPT [8410:1181549]

*filter
:INPUT ACCEPT [309:33618]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [147:14813]
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 8080 -j ACCEPT
-A FORWARD -d 10.1.1.155/32 -p tcp -m state --state NEW,RELATED,ESTABLISHED -m tcp --dport 8080 -j ACCEPT

$ip route

### Post by nickos on 2012-01-04
to port forward your server there is an excellent site called portforward.com select your modem and it will provide you with a full list of information on how to get the job done ;)

---

### Post by Doug S on 2012-01-04
What do you get for this command?```
cat /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward
```It should be 1, if not do this:```
echo 1 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward
```
 
Edit: perhaps also show the counters so we can observe which rules are actually executed:```
sudo iptables-save -c
```And maybe some help is needed from tcpdump or wireshark to observe what is happening at the packet level.

---
